question: Does the R1 inline-profitability patch fix ONLY the pytorch_unet stencil regression (class A), or ALSO the transformer reduction regressions (class B)?
answer: NO — R1 covers class A only. Class-B codegen is BYTE-IDENTICAL under the patch (modulo cache-path comments); timings are within noise of stock. U25 needs an additional cost-model term for the nk-unchanged case before landing.

provenance.hardware: 4x B200, SM clocks locked 1852MHz (bench_parallel --lock-clocks default on)
provenance.method: PYTHONPATH shadow worktrees at HEAD daa79cd25ca: wt_stock (pristine), wt_r1 (HEAD + R1_inline_profitability_gate_fix.patch), wt_off (HEAD + inline_recomputable_producers=False). Built .so/lib/bin/version.py symlinked from /tmp/pytorch-work. One bench_parallel invocation per arm, --all-shapes --gpus 0,1,2,3, fresh TORCHINDUCTOR_CACHE_DIR per arm. Codegen comparison + R1_DEBUG decision trace via results/regression_investigation/bench_repro_arm.py (locked tool).
provenance.note_env_var: TORCHINDUCTOR_INLINE_RECOMPUTABLE_PRODUCERS env var does NOT override the config (verified); arm_stock_repeat.json is that run, kept as a stock repeatability check (matches arm_stock within 0.1-0.8us).

pointwise_457ec7dce9c1.verdict: FIXED by R1 (3.40x, = pass-off level); confirms patch was applied and active

any_amax_amax_af50781fc699.verdict: NOT fixed; regression (1.33x vs off) fully intact under R1

sum_68fcffe5c7fb.verdict: NOT fixed; regression (1.43x vs off) fully intact under R1

sum_02fe2c82b1fd.verdict: NOT fixed; regression (1.33x vs off) fully intact under R1

pointwise_07c6ea330ab4.verdict: win PRESERVED under R1 (still inlines, 1.27x faster than pass-off)

codegen_identity_check: grep -v '# kernel path:' md5sum: stock vs r1 content-identical for all three class-B repros; the only diff lines are tempdir cache paths in comments.

why_r1_cannot_reach_class_B.evidence: R1_DEBUG trace of _is_inline_profitable in the patched arm (dbg/DBG_*).
why_r1_cannot_reach_class_B.mechanism: R1 prices replayed producer-input loads as extra_loads = consumer_numel * stencil_reads - producer_numel. Every class-B inline decision has stencil_reads = 1 (the consumer reads the producer buffer at exactly ONE position — no stencil), and for the full-size consumer consumer_numel == producer_numel, so extra_loads = 0 and the R1 term adds nothing. For the reduction consumer, consumer_numel (post-reduction, e.g. 131072) << producer_numel (67108864), so extra_loads is clamped to 0. Every class-B decision still returns inline=True with identical costs to stock.

traced_decisions.any_amax_amax_af50781fc699: buf0(prod 67.1M, ops 6, nbr 2) and buf8(prod 67.1M, ops 29, nbr 2) each inlined into 2 consumers (reduction cons=131072 and full-size epilogue cons=67.1M), all stencil_reads=1, all inline=True under R1
traced_decisions.sum_68fcffe5c7fb: buf0(prod 8.39M, ops 19, nbr 4) into cons 65536 + cons 8.39M; buf2(ops 15) into cons 8.39M; all stencil_reads=1, inline=True
traced_decisions.sum_02fe2c82b1fd: buf2(prod 524288 -> cons 8.39M, nbr 0) and buf3(prod 8.39M, nbr 1) into cons 65536 + 8.39M; stencil_reads=1, inline=True

what_distinguishes_class_B_from_the_wins.structure: Class B inlines a full-size producer into a REDUCTION consumer that also has a full-size post-reduction epilogue re-reading the same producer. Two independent damage channels, both unpriced by R1: (1) multi-consumer recompute — the producer is inlined into >=2 consumers (the reduction accumulator AND the epilogue), so its full-size input loads are duplicated once per consumer; _is_inline_profitable evaluates each consumer in isolation and credits the full write+read memory_saved to each decision, though the buffer is eliminated only once. (2) persistent->looped reduction downgrade — with the producer materialized (pass off), the fused reduction compiles as a single-pass persistent_reduction (XLNet: triton_per, num_load=2; XGLM: triton_per, num_load=7); with it inlined, the kernel becomes a multi-pass triton_red (XLNet: 3 r0 loops, num_load=6; XGLM: 2 r0 loops, num_load=10) that re-streams the full-size inputs once per r0 pass. Same kernel count, ~1.3-1.4x more HBM traffic.
what_distinguishes_class_B_from_the_wins.win_contrast: The Longformer win (pointwise_07c6ea330ab4) inlines a producer into a single pointwise-style consumer pass: one consumer body, no reduction-loop replay, so eliminating the 75MB intermediate is a pure save (19.2 -> 15.1us).
what_distinguishes_class_B_from_the_wins.suggested_cost_terms: For U25 to land: (a) price producer input re-loads per r0 pass when the consumer is a non-persistent reduction (or when inlining pushes it over the persistent-reduction threshold — the XGLM kernel flipped per->red exactly because of the inline), and (b) account multi-consumer inlining: divide memory_saved across consumers (buffer eliminated once) and sum the recompute/reload cost across all consumers of the producer.

bottom_line: R1 covers class B: NO. Verdict for U25: NOT 'apply R1 and land'. R1 is necessary (fixes class A 3.4x, preserves the wins) but not sufficient — landing needs an additional cost-model term for the nk-unchanged reduction case (multi-consumer + persistent-reduction-loss pricing, per above).
